revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Usa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Tumulus. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century burial mound grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the miqqle of the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[:en:Cuirass|Cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[:en:Cuirass|Cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[:en:Cuirass|Cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[:en:Cuirass|Cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound [[:en:Cuirass|Cuirass]] and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:Yanagigaura Station|Yanagigaura Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound {{ill|Cuirass|en|Cuirass|WD=Q182731|lt=Cuirass}} and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Yanagigaura Station|en|Yanagigaura Station|WD=Q5360192|lt=Yanagigaura Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620871]] Wikidata name: Kuzuhara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound {{ill|Cuirass|en|Cuirass|WD=Q182731|lt=Cuirass}} and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Yanagigaura Station|en|Yanagigaura Station|WD=Q5360192|lt=Yanagigaura Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa|1=Usa, Ōita}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound {{ill|Cuirass|en|Cuirass|WD=Q182731|lt=Cuirass|1=Cuirass}} and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Yanagigaura Station|en|Yanagigaura Station|WD=Q5360192|lt=Yanagigaura Station|1=Yanagigaura Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuzuhara Kofun]][[ja:葛原古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kuzuhara Kofun | native_name =葛原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kuzuhara Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kuzuhara Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kuzuhara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.540611|N|131.336111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|qid=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|26.2|N|131|20|10|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuzuhara Kofun'''|葛原古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onizuka, Kuzuhara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|qid=Q114147|lt=Usa|1=Usa, Ōita}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162618|title=葛原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kuzuhara Kofun is located in a wide open rice field on a low plateau on the left bank of the Yakan River, and was once called the Onizuka Kofun. It is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', with a diameter of 53 meters and height of 6 meters built in two stages. However, there is also a theory that its was originally a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which has lost its anterior portion. It is estimated to have been constructed from the mid to late 5th century. The top of the tumulus is a flat surface with a diameter of 10 meters, with the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] exposed almost in the center. This was discovered by villagers in 1889 when they were clearing the top of the tumulus. The burial chamber has its main axis running north–south, and measures 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1.2 meters deep. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', ironware, and armor, but the armor is particularly noteworthy, and includes a triangular plate leather-bound {{ill|Cuirass|en|Cuirass|qid=Q182731|lt=Cuirass|1=Cuirass}} and a helmet, which is also accompanied by a neck armor. These are typical 5th century Kofun grave goods, but as they are different from that found in the nearby Kawabe-Takamori Kofun cluster, which was constructed from the end of the 3rd century to the middleof the 6th century, it is assumed that this tumulus was built a new ruling clan, possibly from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|Yanagigaura Station|en|Yanagigaura Station|qid=Q5360192|lt=Yanagigaura Station|1=Yanagigaura Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://oita-digitalzukan.jp/cultural_property/葛原古墳/ Oita Digital Cultural Properties Encyclopaedia ]{{in lang|ja}}
